coaches.name: DevZel
coaches.bio: Analytical master. Breaks down complex positions with cold precision.

brilliant: A deeply calculated move that most players would miss.
The engine agrees — this was the only correct path forward.
Exceptional calculation. This changes the entire evaluation.
best: Precise play. You found the engine's top choice.
Clean and accurate. Exactly what the position demanded.
good: A solid choice. Not the absolute best, but well within reason.
This maintains the balance. A logical decision.
Good enough. You kept your advantage intact.
inaccuracy: A slight imprecision. The evaluation shifted a bit here.
Not the cleanest move. A more accurate option existed.
This loses some of the edge you had built up.
mistake: This is where the game started to slip.
A concrete error. The opponent now has real chances.
The position is now objectively difficult to defend.
blunder: A critical error that changes the game fundamentally.
This move drops significant material or loses the game.
The engine shows a decisive advantage for the opponent now.

coaches.name: Kimmy
coaches.bio: Friendly guide for beginners. Makes chess fun and approachable!

brilliant: Wow, amazing move! You really surprised everyone! 🌟
That's like a magic trick! Super clever thinking!
Oh my gosh, I didn't even see that coming! Brilliant!
best: Great job! You picked the very best move!
Perfect! The computer totally agrees with you!
Yay! You found exactly the right move!
good: Nice move! That was a smart choice!
Good thinking! You kept your pieces safe.
Well done! You're playing really well!
inaccuracy: Oops, a tiny slip! But don't worry, you're still okay!
Almost perfect! There was a slightly better option though.
That's okay! Just a small mistake. Keep going!
mistake: Oh no, that one hurt a little! The other player got some advantage.
Hmm, that wasn't quite right. Let's learn from it!
A bit of a stumble there! But you can come back!
blunder: Uh oh! That was a big oopsie! The other player is really happy now!
Oh dear! That move gave away too much!
Eek! That's going to make things very hard now!

coaches.name: Digger
coaches.bio: Tactical excavator. Digs deep into combinations and hidden patterns.

brilliant: DEEP LINES. This move requires calculating 8+ moves ahead. Respect.
The tactical shot that ends the game. Pure calculation dominance.
Hidden resource unearthed. This is what deep analysis looks like.
best: Engine-approved. The sharpest continuation in this position.
Tactical precision. No room for alternatives here.
The forcing move that keeps everything on track.
good: Solid. Maintains the tactical tension without overreaching.
A reasonable choice that doesn't blunder anything concrete.
Keeps the complications alive. Good practical play.
inaccuracy: A tempo lost. Tactically, there was something sharper available.
The tactical sequence has been slightly disrupted here.
Not wrong, but the opportunity for something stronger was missed.
mistake: This move walks into a tactical problem.
The opponent now has a concrete sequence to exploit.
Pattern detected: this allows a tactical refutation.
blunder: TACTICAL CATASTROPHE. A forced sequence wins for the opponent.
Walked into a combination. The position is now resignable.
Classic blunder pattern. This decides the game immediately.

coaches.name: Rj
coaches.bio: Attack! Attack! Attack! Rj believes the best defense is overwhelming offense.

brilliant: FIRE MOVE. Pure aggression rewarded. The king has nowhere to hide!
This is what ATTACKING chess looks like. Absolute weapon.
No mercy. This move goes straight for the throat!
best: Sharp and aggressive. Exactly the kind of chess I want to see!
Attack maintained! The pressure never lets up!
This keeps ALL the threats alive. Beautiful chaos!
good: Okay move, keeps some attacking chances alive.
Decent. Could be sharper but the attack continues.
Fine. You're still in the game and pressing forward.
inaccuracy: You let them breathe! Should have kept the pressure on!
A defensive move when you should have been attacking!
Lost a beat. The attack lost some of its momentum here.
mistake: You gave them time to organize their defense. Big mistake.
This allows the opponent to counterattack. NOT acceptable!
The attack has stalled. This is how games are lost!
blunder: DISASTER! The entire attack just collapsed!
You handed them the game! How do you let this happen?!
The king was RIGHT THERE and you walked away. Unbelievable!

coaches.name: Kim
coaches.bio: Balanced educator. Teaches principles, not just moves.

brilliant: This demonstrates deep understanding of positional sacrifices.
A move that combines tactics with long-term strategic vision.
Excellent! This shows mastery of both calculation and intuition.
best: Principled play. You followed solid chess fundamentals here.
The position called for this — and you delivered it.
Well-reasoned move. Control, activity, and safety all balanced.
good: A reasonable continuation. You applied good chess principles.
Solid choice. You kept the position manageable.
Good move. You didn't create any unnecessary weaknesses.
inaccuracy: A small lesson here: the principle of piece activity suggests a better square existed.
Not terrible, but let's examine what the position was asking for.
A tiny drift from optimal. Worth reviewing the alternative.
mistake: This is a teaching moment. The position required a different approach.
An instructive error. Let's understand WHY the engine prefers another move.
This breaks a key principle. A good pattern to learn from.
blunder: A critical mistake. Let's use this as a lesson for future games.
This shows why material count alone doesn't tell the whole story.
An important learning moment. The position gave clear warning signs.
